paper_title: The Synchrosqueezing algorithm for time-varying spectral analysis: Robustness properties and new paleoclimate applications

methods.method_name: Comparison of Synchrosqueezing with Reassignment Techniques
methods.description: This method compares the Synchrosqueezing algorithm with two common time-frequency reassignment (TFR) methods: the spectrogram and the Wigner-Ville distribution. The comparison is performed on a signal with increased noise to evaluate the robustness and clarity of the Synchrosqueezing transform.

steps.description: Apply the Synchrosqueezing transform to the signal s(t) for t ∈ [2,8] with increased noise (σ 2 = 5).
steps.searchable_summary: apply Synchrosqueezing transform to noisy signal

steps.description: Apply the spectrogram and Wigner-Ville distribution to the same signal and noise conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: apply spectrogram and Wigner-Ville distribution to noisy signal

steps.description: Visualize the results of the Synchrosqueezing, spectrogram, and Wigner-Ville distribution in Fig. 4.
Analyze the TF plots to compare the presence of spurious components and noise clutter.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze TF plots for spurious components and noise
outputs: analysis of spurious components and noise
